name: qubit-measurement-analysis

metadata.name: qubit-measurement-analysis
metadata.summary: Tools for analyzing in-phase / quadrature signals from superconducting quantum systems

documentation:
# IQ-processing
Tools for analyzing in-phase / quadrature signals

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.shot_collection_plotter
documentation:
Shot Collection plotting functionality.

This module provides visualization capabilities for collections of quantum measurement shots,
including scatter plots and histogram plots with customizable styling.

classes.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.shot_collection_plotter.CollectionPlotter

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.shot_collection_plotter.CollectionPlotter.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the plotter.

Args:
    children: ShotCollection instance containing the data to plot

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.shot_collection_plotter.CollectionPlotter._prepare_plot
documentation:
Prepare plotting environment.

Args:
    ax: Matplotlib axes to plot on

Returns:
    Matplotlib axes for plotting

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.shot_collection_plotter.CollectionPlotter._prepare_histogram_data
documentation:
Prepare data for histogram plotting.

Args:
    counts: Dictionary of counts or probabilities
    correct_key: Key to highlight
    correct_color: Color for highlighted key
    default_color: Default bar color

Returns:
    Tuple of (labels, values, colors)

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.shot_collection_plotter.CollectionPlotter.scatter
documentation:
Create a scatter plot of the measurement data.

Creates a scatter plot where each point represents a measurement value,
with automatic grouping by qubit and state.

Args:
    ax: Matplotlib axes to plot on. If None, creates new figure
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to scatter:
        - marker: Custom marker style
        - label: Custom label for legend
        - color: Point color
        - alpha: Point transparency
        - s: Point size
        - etc.

Returns:
    The matplotlib axes containing the plot

Example:
    >>> plotter.scatter(color='blue', alpha=0.5)

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.shot_collection_plotter.CollectionPlotter.plot_hist
documentation:
Create a histogram of shot counts.

Creates a bar plot showing the count of shots for each state.

Args:
    ax: Matplotlib axes to plot on. If None, creates new figure
    correct_key: State to highlight
    correct_color: Color for highlighted state
    default_color: Default bar color

Returns:
    The matplotlib axes containing the plot

Example:
    >>> plotter.plot_hist(correct_key='0', correct_color='green')

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.shot_collection_plotter.CollectionPlotter.plot_hist_proba
documentation:
Create a histogram of shot probabilities.

Creates a bar plot showing the probability distribution of states.

Args:
    ax: Matplotlib axes to plot on. If None, creates new figure
    correct_key: State to highlight
    correct_color: Color for highlighted state
    default_color: Default bar color

Returns:
    The matplotlib axes containing the plot

Example:
    >>> plotter.plot_hist_proba(correct_key='0', correct_color='green')

Plotter class for visualizing collections of quantum measurement shots.

This class provides methods for creating scatter plots and histograms
of shot collections, with support for customizable styling and automatic
state labeling.

Attributes:
    children: The ShotCollection instance to plot

classes.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.base_plotter.BasicShotPlotter

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.base_plotter.BasicShotPlotter.scatter_matplotlib
documentation: Generate a basic scatter plot using Matplotlib.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.base_plotter.BasicShotPlotter.plot_matplotlib
documentation: Generate a basic line plot using Matplotlib
Class for generating basic plots.

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.single_shot_plotter
documentation:
Single-Shot plotting functionality.

This module provides plotting capabilities for single-shot quantum measurements,
including scatter plots and line plots with customizable styling.

classes.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.single_shot_plotter.SingleShotPlotter

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.single_shot_plotter.SingleShotPlotter.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the plotter.

Args:
    children: SingleShot instance containing the data to plot

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.single_shot_plotter.SingleShotPlotter._prepare_plot
documentation:
Prepare plotting environment.

Args:
    ax: Matplotlib axes to plot on
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for plotting

Returns:
    Tuple of (matplotlib axes, copied kwargs dict)

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.single_shot_plotter.SingleShotPlotter.scatter
documentation:
Create a scatter plot of the measurement data.

Creates a scatter plot where each point represents a measurement value,
with optional automatic state labeling for demodulated data.

Args:
    ax: Matplotlib axes to plot on. If None, creates new figure
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to scatter:
        - marker: Custom marker style
        - label: Custom label for legend
        - color: Point color
        - alpha: Point transparency
        - s: Point size
        - etc.

Returns:
    The matplotlib axes containing the plot

Example:
    >>> plotter.scatter(color='blue', alpha=0.5)

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.visualization.single_shot_plotter.SingleShotPlotter.plot
documentation:
Create a line plot of the measurement data.

Creates a line plot showing the real and imaginary components
of the measurement values over time or custom x-coordinates.

Args:
    ax: Matplotlib axes to plot on. If None, creates new figure
    x: Optional x-coordinates for the plot
    in_phase: If to plot real valued component
    quadrature: If to plot imag valued component
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to plot:
        - label: Custom label(s) for legend
        - color: Line color(s)
        - linestyle: Line style
        - linewidth: Line width
        - marker: Point marker
        - etc.

Returns:
    The matplotlib axes containing the plot

Example:
    >>> plotter.plot(linestyle='--', marker='o')

Plotter class for visualizing single-shot quantum measurements.

This class provides methods for creating scatter plots and line plots
of single-shot measurement data, with support for customizable styling
and automatic qubit state labeling.

Attributes:
    children: The SingleShot instance to plot

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data._distance_utils
documentation: Utility functions for distance calculations in qubit measurement analysis.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data._distance_utils.sspd_to
documentation:
Compute SSPD from source to target.

Args:
    self_values: The values of the source.
    other: The target to compute SSPD to.
    xp: The ArrayModule instance for computation.
    method: The method to use for SSPD computation.
           'cross_product' (default): Compute full distance matrix
           'pairwise': Compute distances between corresponding pairs
           'self_cross_product': Compute upper triangular part of self-distance matrix

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data._distance_utils._compute_sspd
documentation:
Args:
    source: array of shape (N1, ch, L1) or (ch, L1)
    target: array of shape (N2, ch, L2) or (ch, L2)
    xp: array module instance
    method: 'pairwise' or 'cross_product'

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd
documentation:
SSPD (Symmetric Segment-Path Distance) implementation.

This module provides functions to calculate the Symmetric Segment-Path Distance
between trajectories.

Reference:
    P. Besse, B. Guillouet, J.-M. Loubes, and R. Francois,
    "Review and perspective for distance based trajectory clustering,"
    arXiv preprint arXiv:1508.04904, 2015.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd.point_to_point
documentation:
Calculate the Euclidean distance between two complex points.

Args:
    p1 (np.complex64): The first point.
    p2 (np.complex64): The second point.

Returns:
    float: The Euclidean distance between p1 and p2.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd.point_to_segment
documentation:
Calculate the minimum distance from a point to a line segment.

Args:
    p (np.complex64): The point.
    seg_a (np.complex64): The start point of the line segment.
    seg_b (np.complex64): The end point of the line segment.

Returns:
    float: The minimum distance from the point to the line segment.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd.point_to_trajectory
documentation:
Calculate the minimum distance from a point to a trajectory.

Args:
    p (np.complex64): The point.
    array (np.ndarray): The trajectory represented as an array of complex points.

Returns:
    float: The minimum distance from the point to the trajectory.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd.segment_path_distance
documentation:
Calculate the segment path distance from one trajectory to another.

The segment path distance is the average of the minimum distances
from each point in the first trajectory to the second trajectory.

Args:
    trajectory (np.ndarray): The trajectory.
    target_trajectory (np.ndarray): The target trajectory.

Returns:
    float: The segment path distance from trajectory to target_trajectory.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd.symmetrized_segment_path_distance
documentation:
Calculate the symmetrized segment path distance between two trajectories.

The symmetrized segment path distance is the average of the segment path
distance from trajectory to other_trajectory and from other_trajectory to trajectory.

Args:
    trajectory (np.ndarray): The first trajectory.
    other_trajectory (np.ndarray): The second trajectory.

Returns:
    float: The symmetrized segment path distance between trajectory and other_trajectory.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd.cross_product
documentation:
Compute SSPD from M trajectories to N target trajectories.

Args:
    trajectories (np.ndarray): Array of shape (M, L) containing M trajectories of length L.
    target_trajectories (np.ndarray): Array of shape (N, L) containing N target trajectories of length L.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Distance matrix of shape (M, N) containing SSPD between all pairs of trajectories.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd.pairwise
documentation:
Compute SSPD from trajectories to their corresponding target trajectories.

Args:
    trajectories (np.ndarray): Array of shape (N, L1) containing N trajectories of length L1.
    target_trajectories (np.ndarray): Array of shape (N, L2) containing N target trajectories of length L2.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Array of shape (N,) containing SSPD between corresponding pairs of trajectories.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._sspd.self_cross_product
documentation:
Compute SSPD between all pairs of trajectories, returning only the upper triangular part.

Args:
    trajectories (np.ndarray): Array of shape (N, L) containing N trajectories of length L.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Flattened upper triangular part of the SSPD matrix, shape (N*(N-1)/2,).
    The vector is ordered as [d(0,1), d(0,2), ..., d(0,n-1), d(1,2), ..., d(1,n-1), ..., d(n-2,n-1)]

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module
documentation:
Array module for unified CPU/GPU array operations.

This module provides a unified interface for array operations across CPU (NumPy)
and GPU (CuPy) backends, with automatic handling of device selection and memory
management.

classes.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.Transformations
documentation:
Container for array transformation operations.

Holds references to various array transformation functions that can be executed
on either CPU or GPU depending on the parent ArrayModule's device setting.

classes.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule._init_cpu_backend
documentation: Initialize CPU backend using NumPy and SciPy.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule._init_cuda_backend
documentation: Initialize CUDA backend using CuPy.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule.__getattr__
documentation: Delegate attribute access to numpy module.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule.__repr__
documentation: String representation of the ArrayModule.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule.free_all_blocks
documentation: Free all memory blocks when using CUDA backend.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule.dtype
documentation: Default dtype for array operations.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule._get_sspd_module
documentation: Load appropriate SSPD implementation based on device and optimization settings.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis._array_module.ArrayModule._get_transformations_module
documentation: Load and initialize transformation functions based on device and optimization settings.

Unified interface for CPU/GPU array operations.

Provides a consistent interface for array operations that can be executed on either
CPU (using NumPy) or GPU (using CuPy) with automatic device management.

Args:
    device: Target device for computations ("cpu" or "cuda[:device_id]")
    use_cython: Whether to use Cython-optimized implementations when available

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data.single_shot
documentation:
Data processing functionality for Single-Shots.

This module provides the SingleShot class for storing and processing single-shot
measurement data in quantum experiments.

documentation:
Initialize a SingleShot instance.

Args:
    value: Complex-valued measurement data
    qubits_classes: Mapping of qubit indices to their states
    _is_demodulated: Flag indicating if data has been demodulated
    device: Computation device ("cpu" or "cuda")
    _id: Unique identifier for the shot
    _use_cython: Flag indicating if to use Cython compiled operations

Raises:
    TypeError: If value type doesn't match device or isn't complex
    ValueError: If value dimensions are invalid

Args:

    subfolder: Subfolder name ('train', 'val', or 'test')
    index: File index to load
    verbose: Whether to print load information

Returns:
    Loaded SingleShot instance

Raises:
    ValueError: If loaded file has unsupported dtype

A class for storing and processing a single SingleShot entity.

This class represents a single-shot measurement in quantum experiments,
providing methods for data manipulation, analysis, and visualization.

Attributes:
    value (NDArray): Complex-valued measurement data
    _qubits_classes (Dict[int, int]): Mapping of qubit indices to their states
    _is_demodulated (bool): Flag indicating if data has been demodulated
    xp (ArrayModule): Array processing module (CPU/GPU)
    id (str): Unique identifier for the shot
    _plotter (SingleShotPlotter): Visualization handler

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cython.classification.temporal_post_processor
documentation:
Python wrapper for Cython-accelerated temporal post-processor implementation

@article{2310.18519,
Author = {Saeed A. Khan and Ryan Kaufman and Boris Mesits and Michael Hatridge and Hakan E. Türeci},
Title = {Practical Trainable Temporal Postprocessor for Multistate Quantum Measurement},
Year = {2023},
Eprint = {arXiv:2310.18519},
Howpublished = {PRX Quantum 5, 020364 (2024)},
Doi = {10.1103/PRXQuantum.5.020364},
}

classes.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cython.classification.temporal_post_processor.CTPPSingle

Fit the temporal post-processor to the data using Cython-optimized computations.

Parameters
----------
data : ShotCollection
    Collection of shots with class labels

documentation:
Predict classes for new data using Cython-optimized computations.

Parameters
----------
data : Union[ShotCollection, SingleShot]
    Input data to classify

Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    Predicted class labels
Cython-accelerated version of TPPSingle.

classes.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cython.classification.temporal_post_processor.CTPP

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cython.classification.temporal_post_processor.CTPP.fit
documentation:
Fit temporal post-processors for each qubit using Cython-optimized computations.

Parameters
----------
data : ShotCollection
    Collection of shots with class labels for multiple qubits

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cython.classification.temporal_post_processor.CTPP.predict
documentation:
Predict classes for new data across all qubits using Cython-optimized computations.

Parameters
----------
data : Union[ShotCollection, SingleShot]
    Input data to classify

Returns
-------
Union[ShotCollection, SingleShot]
    Classified shots
Cython-accelerated version of TPP.

Returns:
    Self with pending demodulation

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data.shot_collection.ShotCollection.mean_centring_all
documentation: Center all shots by subtracting mean.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data.shot_collection.ShotCollection.mean_convolve_all
documentation: Apply mean convolution to all shots.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data.shot_collection.ShotCollection.whittaker_eilers_smoother_all
documentation: Apply Whittaker-Eilers smoothing to all shots.

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data.shot_collection.ShotCollection.compute
documentation:
Execute all pending operations.

Args:
    free_all_blocks: Whether to free memory after computation

Returns:
    New ShotCollection with operations applied

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data.shot_collection.ShotCollection.sspd_to
documentation:
Calculate SSPD to another shot/array.

Args:
    other: Target to compute distance to
    method: Distance computation method

Returns:
    Array of SSPD values

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data.shot_collection.ShotCollection.save_all
documentation:
Save collection with train/val/test splits.

Args:
    parent_dir: Parent directory for saving
    train_ratio: Fraction for training set
    val_ratio: Fraction for validation set
    test_ratio: Fraction for test set
    clear_existing: Whether to clear existing files
    verbose: Whether to print progress

Raises:
    ValueError: If ratios are invalid

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.data.shot_collection.ShotCollection.load
documentation:
Load shots from directory.

Args:
    parent_dir: Parent directory containing data
    qubits_dir: Qubit number set folder (e.g., '123')
    state: System state (e.g., '001')
    subfolder: Subfolder name ('train', 'val', or 'test')
    num_samples: Number of samples to load (None for all)
    verbose: Whether to print progress

Returns:
    New ShotCollection with loaded shots

A class for managing and processing collections of single-shot measurements.

This class provides functionality for handling multiple SingleShot instances,
including batch operations, filtering, and statistical analysis.

Attributes:
    singleshots (List[SingleShot]): List of SingleShot instances
    xp (ArrayModule): Array processing module (CPU/GPU)
    all_values (NDArray): Combined array of all shot values
    all_qubits_classes (List[Dict]): List of qubit class mappings
    _is_demodulated (bool): Demodulation status of shots
    _plotter (CollectionPlotter): Visualization handler
    _ops (List): Pending lazy operations

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cli
documentation: Command-line interface for qubit_measurement_analysis package.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cli.main
documentation: Main CLI entry point.

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cuda.spd.cross_product
documentation:
Computes SPD from M trajectories to N target trajectories.
Returns (M, N) distance matrix
trajectories: 2d cupy array with M trajectories
target_trajectories: 2d cupy array with N trajectories

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cuda.spd.pairwise
documentation: Computes SPD from trajectories to their corresponding target trajectories

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cuda.sspd.pairwise
documentation: Computes SSPD from trajectories to their corresponding other trajectories

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cuda.sspd.cross_product
documentation:
Computes SSPD from M trajectories to N other trajectories.
Returns (M, N) distance matrix

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.cuda.sspd.self_cross_product
documentation:
Computes SSPD between all pairs of trajectories, returning only the upper triangular part.

Args:
    trajectories: Array of shape (N, L) containing N trajectories of length L.

Returns:
    Flattened upper triangular part of the SSPD matrix, shape (N*(N-1)/2,).
    The vector is ordered as [d(0,1), d(0,2), ..., d(0,n-1), d(1,2), ..., d(1,n-1), ..., d(n-2,n-1)]

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.gaussian_mixture.ExtendedGaussianMixture.relabel_points_ellipses
documentation:
Classify points based on whether they fall into scaled ellipses of GMM components.

Args:
    X (ndarray): ndarray of shape (n_samples, n_features), data to relabel
    y (ndarray): current labels for data
    scaling_factor (float): float, scaling factor for standard deviations along principal axes

Returns:
    ndarray: ndarray of shape (n_samples,), class labels for each point. 'x' for outliers

methods.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.gaussian_mixture.ExtendedGaussianMixture.plot_ellipses
documentation:
Plot ellipses representing the Gaussian components of the GMM.

Args:
    ax: Matplotlib axis object. Defaults to None.
    sd_factor (float, optional): Scaling factor for the standard deviations along principal axes. Defaults to 1.0.

modules.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.temporal_post_processor
documentation:
Semi-analytical temporal post-processor implementation

@article{2310.18519,
Author = {Saeed A. Khan and Ryan Kaufman and Boris Mesits and Michael Hatridge and Hakan E. Türeci},
Title = {Practical Trainable Temporal Postprocessor for Multistate Quantum Measurement},
Year = {2023},
Eprint = {arXiv:2310.18519},
Howpublished = {PRX Quantum 5, 020364 (2024)},
Doi = {10.1103/PRXQuantum.5.020364},
}

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.metrics.calculate_accuracy
documentation:
Calculates the accuracy from the confusion matrix.

Parameters:
- conf_matrix (numpy.ndarray): Confusion matrix

Returns:
- float: Accuracy value

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.metrics.calculate_fidelity
documentation:
Calculates the fidelity from the confusion matrix.
Fidelity is calculated as:
fidelity = 1 - 0.5 * (FP + FN) / (TP + FP + FN + TN)

Parameters:
- conf_matrix (numpy.ndarray): Confusion matrix

Returns:
- float: Fidelity value

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.metrics.calculate_binary_precision
documentation:
Calculates precision for binary classification.

Parameters:
- conf_matrix (numpy.ndarray): Confusion matrix for binary classification

Returns:
- float: Precision for the positive class

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.metrics.calculate_binary_recall
documentation:
Calculates recall for binary classification.

Parameters:
- conf_matrix (numpy.ndarray): Confusion matrix for binary classification

Returns:
- float: Recall for the positive class

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.metrics.calculate_macro_precision
documentation:
Calculates macro-averaged precision for multiclass classification.
Macro-averaged precision is the average of precision values for each class.

Parameters:
- conf_matrix (numpy.ndarray): Confusion matrix for multiclass classification

Returns:
- float: Macro-averaged precision

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.metrics.calculate_macro_recall
documentation:
Calculates macro-averaged recall for multiclass classification.
Macro-averaged recall is the average of recall values for each class.

Parameters:
- conf_matrix (numpy.ndarray): Confusion matrix for multiclass classification

Returns:
- float: Macro-averaged recall

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.metrics.calculate_micro_precision
documentation:
Calculates micro-averaged precision for multiclass classification.
Micro-averaged precision treats all classes equally by considering total TP, FP, and FN.

Parameters:
- conf_matrix (numpy.ndarray): Confusion matrix for multiclass classification

Returns:
- float: Micro-averaged precision

functions.name: qubit_measurement_analysis.classification.metrics.calculate_micro_recall
documentation:
Calculates micro-averaged recall for multiclass classification.
Micro-averaged recall treats all classes equally by considering total TP, FP, and FN.

Parameters:
- conf_matrix (numpy.ndarray): Confusion matrix for multiclass classification

Returns:
- float: Micro-averaged recall
